text:
$POET is starting to move — +75.9% in just 3 days, now pushing ~$12.8.

This isn’t random. Photonics is becoming a key bottleneck in AI infrastructure: faster data transfer, lower power, and critical for next-gen data centers.

If compute was the first wave, photonics could be next.

Price action is starting to reflect it.

$LITE $COHR $AXTI $AAOI

text:
Marvell $MRVL Announced they will be acquiring Polariton Technologies 

They plan to add high speed low power plasmonics based silicon photonics devices to its portfolio

This gives Marvell a stronger presence in optical technology and positions them better for next gen connectivity

This partnership plans to integrate Marvell into Nvidia’s AI Factory 

They also plan to integrate Marvell into AI-RAN ecosystem with NVLink fusion!

The partnership also includes joint development of silicon photonics https://t.co/7lhuDMAxHN

text:
$poet and $sivef indirect benefits to this $mrvl acquisition-

$poet Technologies (POET) sees indirect positive momentum, not a direct tie to Polariton itself.POET’s core tech is its Optical Interposer platform—a wafer-scale, passive-alignment hybrid photonics solution that integrates light sources, modulators, waveguides, etc., into compact optical engines/light engines. It’s upstream in the same AI interconnect value chain Marvell is aggressively building out.
Marvell’s prior Celestial AI acquisition (Dec 2025) already made POET a beneficiary: Celestial used POET’s tech (e.g., Blazar light sources) as a key enabler for its Photonic Fabric. Marvell inherited that supply relationship, and POET has confirmed purchase orders/shipments tied to it.

This new Polariton deal further validates Marvell’s heavy commitment to scaling photonics for AI (now adding plasmonic modulation on top of SiPh). It doesn’t displace POET—it reinforces the ecosystem demand for integrated optical engines. Analysts and investors have repeatedly called out POET as a “real winner” or potential M&A target in the wake of Marvell’s optics moves, as hyperscalers/NVIDIA/Marvell need proven, scalable integration platforms. 

$POET gets another leg of validation and potential order acceleration. It’s not a direct “Polariton synergy” play, but the Marvell optics flywheel helps POET’s positioning in the same AI connectivity wave.

 $sivef is a strong indirect beneficiary and one of the more credible pure-plays in the exact layer this deal targets.Sivers Photonics specializes in high-performance DFB laser arrays, CW (continuous-wave) lasers, and external light source modules explicitly designed for AI data-center optical interconnects, CPO, pluggables, and SiPh integration.
They have established partnerships in the ecosystem (e.g., with POET for light engines, Ayar Labs, and paths into Marvell/Celestial/NVIDIA optics roadmaps via the CW-WDM MSA and related collaborations). Their lasers are a critical “fuel” for SiPh-based modulators and engines—like the plasmonic + SiPh solutions Marvell just acquired.
This deal accelerates adoption of exactly the architectures Sivers supplies (1.6T+ LRO transceivers, CPO, ultra-low-power AI interconnects). Recent Sivers news (e.g., Jabil collaboration on 1.6T modules) already shows momentum; Marvell’s move pours more fuel on the fire for laser array demand.

engines/light engines. It’s upstream in the same AI interconnect value chain Marvell is aggressively building out. Marvell’s prior Celestial AI acquisition (Dec 2025) already made POET a beneficiary: Celestial used POET’s tech (e.g., Blazar light sources) as a key enabler for its Photonic Fabric. Marvell inherited that supply relationship, and POET has confirmed purchase orders/shipments tied to it. This new Polariton deal further validates Marvell’s heavy commitment to scaling photonics for AI (now adding plasmonic modulation on top of SiPh). It doesn’t displace POET—it reinforces the ecosystem demand for integrated optical engines. Analysts and investors have repeatedly called out POET as a “real winner” or potential M&A target in the wake of Marvell’s optics moves, as hyperscalers/NVIDIA/Marvell need proven, scalable integration platforms. $POET gets another leg of validation and potential order acceleration. Their lasers are a critical “fuel” for SiPh-based modulators and engines—like the plasmonic + SiPh solutions Marvell just acquired. This deal accelerates adoption of exactly the architectures Sivers supplies (1.6T+ LRO transceivers, CPO, ultra-low-power AI interconnects). text:
$GFS GlobalFoundries 

定位:AI 数据中心 → 光互联 → 硅光(Silicon Photonics)代工厂

坐标系:
光引擎里的两种主流技术路线:一种基于磷化铟(InP,做激光最好,也是 Sivers / AXT 的主场),另一种基于硅(硅光 / SiPh,做调制和波导最适合 CMOS 量产)。

硅光的思路是"把光学器件用成熟半导体工艺做在硅晶圆上",好处是能借用半导体行业几十年积累的产能、良率、成本曲线。问题是硅本身不会发光,所以硅光要和 InP 激光配合使用 —— 这是 POET 这类"封装平台"存在的原因。

硅光需要专门的工艺和专门的晶圆厂。GlobalFoundries 就是这个赛道的最大代工厂。

公司背景:
美国 Malta, NY 总部,全球纯代工厂第三名(TSMC、Samsung 之后,和 UMC、SMIC 同级)。不做最先进制程(3nm/5nm 是 TSMC 的地盘),专做 12–28nm 的特殊工艺节点 —— 射频、模拟混合信号、功率管理、SOI、以及硅光。

2025 年 11 月收购了新加坡 AMF(Advanced Micro Foundry),这是全球第一家商业化硅光纯代工厂,拥有 200mm 硅光平台。

加上之前收购的埃及 InfiniLink(光学数据连接芯片创业公司),GlobalFoundries 宣称自己是全球最大的纯代工硅光晶圆厂。

技术路线:
纽约工厂做 300mm 硅光,新加坡(AMF)做 200mm 硅光,两地互补。

GF 同时在做 InP 激光和硅 CMOS 的单片异构集成,长期目标是做出"一块晶圆上同时有激光、调制器、驱动、波导"的完全集成光引擎。

赛道位置:
硅光代工这个环节格局是:Intel 做自用的(自己的 CPO 产品)、TSMC 给 Broadcom 做、GlobalFoundries 是最大的纯代工(向所有人开放)。

Broadcom、Marvell、Cisco、Coherent、Lumentum 这些公司的硅光芯片,相当一部分会在 GF 的晶圆厂生产。

它的地位相当于硅光赛道的"TSMC" —— 不做自己品牌产品,做所有人的代工。

财务和公司状态:
市值约 200 亿美金,2026 年硅光业务预计贡献 AMF 收购后 +7500 万美金,目标 2030 年前硅光年收入突破 10 亿美金。

硅光市场本身以 25% CAGR 增长。

主体业务(非硅光)还是传统代工,受汽车和工业需求拖累,财务表现平淡。2026 年 3 月做了二次公开发行 + 并行股份回购,市场反应一般。

所以 GFS 的股价走势更像"被硅光故事带动的大盘股",不是纯粹硅光标的。

AI 周期里的角色:
"硅光代工"的稀缺标的。

想直接投硅光,美股里几乎没有纯正选项(Ayar Labs 未上市,Lightmatter 未上市),GFS 是公开市场最直接的硅光赋能者 —— 但要意识到它的非硅光业务占比还很大,硅光故事被稀释。

核心赌注:
硅光在 2027–2030 年真正从 CPO 需求起量;
GF 在开放代工市场能拿住大部分份额;
主体代工业务不继续恶化。

仓位定位:
硅光主题的代理持仓(proxy play),不是纯正选项。

和 POET / Sivers 形成互补:POET / Sivers 是"如果硅光/CPO 跑通 10 倍股"的押注,GFS 是"硅光跑通它至少有稳定订单"的底仓。

适合追求硅光敞口但又不想承担 early-stage 波动的配置。

关键观察点:
AMF 整合进度和新加坡硅光产能爬坡 → 
Broadcom / Marvell 等大客户的硅光订单走向 → 
主体代工业务汽车/工业板块能否止跌。

硅光的卖铲人。

{"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "$GFS GlobalFoundries 定位:AI 数据中心 → 光互联 → 硅光(Silicon Photonics)代工厂 坐标系: 光引擎里的两种主流技术路线:一种基于磷化铟(InP,做激光最好,也是 Sivers / AXT 的主场),另一种基于硅(硅光 / SiPh,做调制和波导最适合 CMOS 量产)。 硅光的思路是"把光学器件用成熟半导体工艺做在硅晶圆上",好处是能借用半导体行业几十年积累的产能、良率、成本曲线。问题是硅本身不会发光,所以硅光要和 InP 激光配合使用 —— 这是 POET 这类"封装平台"存在的原因。 硅光需要专门的工艺和专门的晶圆厂。GlobalFoundries 就是这个赛道的最大代工厂。 公司背景: 美国 Malta, NY 总部,全球纯代工厂第三名(TSMC、Samsung 之后,和 UMC、SMIC 同级)。不做最先进制程(3nm/5nm 是 TSMC 的地盘),专做 12–28nm 的特殊工艺节点 —— 射频、模拟混合信号、功率管理、SOI、以及硅光。 2025 年 11 月收购了新加坡 AMF(Advanced Micro Foundry),这是全球第一家商业化硅光纯代工厂,拥有 200mm 硅光平台。 加上之前收购的埃及 InfiniLink(光学数据连接芯片创业公司),GlobalFoundries 宣称自己是全球最大的纯代工硅光晶圆厂。 技术路线: 纽约工厂做 300mm 硅光,新加坡(AMF)做 200mm 硅光,两地互补。 GF 同时在做 InP 激光和硅 CMOS 的单片异构集成,长期目标是做出"一块晶圆上同时有激光、调制器、驱动、波导"的完全集成光引擎。 赛道位置: 硅光代工这个环节格局是:Intel 做自用的(自己的 CPO 产品)、TSMC 给 Broadcom 做、GlobalFoundries 是最大的纯代工(向所有人开放)。 Broadcom、Marvell、Cisco、Coherent、Lumentum 这些公司的硅光芯片,相当一部分会在 GF 的晶圆厂生产。 它的地位相当于硅光赛道的"TSMC" —— 不做自己品牌产品,做所有人的代工。 财务和公司状态: 市值约 200 亿美金,2026 年硅光业务预计贡献 AMF 收购后 +7500 万美金,目标 2030 年前硅光年收入突破 10 亿美金。 硅光市场本身以 25% CAGR 增长。 主体业务(非硅光)还是传统代工,受汽车和工业需求拖累,财务表现平淡。2026 年 3 月做了二次公开发行 + 并行股份回购,市场反应一般。 所以 GFS 的股价走势更像"被硅光故事带动的大盘股",不是纯粹硅光标的。 AI 周期里的角色: "硅光代工"的稀缺标的。 想直接投硅光,美股里几乎没有纯正选项(Ayar Labs 未上市,Lightmatter 未上市),GFS 是公开市场最直接的硅光赋能者 —— 但要意识到它的非硅光业务占比还很大,硅光故事被稀释。 核心赌注: 硅光在 2027–2030 年真正从 CPO 需求起量; GF 在开放代工市场能拿住大部分份额; 主体代工业务不继续恶化。 仓位定位: 硅光主题的代理持仓(proxy play),不是纯正选项。 和 POET / Sivers 形成互补:POET / Sivers 是"如果硅光/CPO 跑通 10 倍股"的押注,GFS 是"硅光跑通它至少有稳定订单"的底仓。 适合追求硅光敞口但又不想承担 early-stage 波动的配置。 关键观察点: AMF 整合进度和新加坡硅光产能爬坡 → Broadcom / Marvell 等大客户的硅光订单走向 → 主体代工业务汽车/工业板块能否止跌。 硅光的卖铲人。"}}}, "rest_id": "2047074469135655314", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "133", text: TXN showing signs that Analog Chip Season may finally be taking off. I’ve been talking about this coming transition in the hardware trade since Q4 of last year and it finally seems like it may be upon us. The first screenshot below hurts my soul, me pumping Photonics in early December but not longing any of them, but that’s the macro tourist game for ya. Regardless, this signals the continuing evolution of the manufacturing boom going on globally. In a time where energy constraints are picking up, any hardware that can bypass those constraints will get bid up here big time, and that is exactly what Analog chips provide, energy efficiency. Listen I know I’ve been banging the drum on potential trouble coming to the AI Datacenter Buildout but I don’t think it is going to break yet or crash the economy yet. As long as NVDA remains elevated, even if flat, capital from that sector will continue to slosh around into different ancillary corners of the same trade. I.E. as NVDA has languished since Q3 of last year, memory stocks have been on a tear. I think we are going to see now a rotation from this existing capital base start going into the Analog semiconductor names. I think as manufacturing output begins to diversify away from AI, I think having exposure to Analog Semis will help buttress any pain from the AI Hardware trade that should come, once demand destruction starts getting actualized. I think I’m going to finally get some exposure to them as I think it is a great asymmetric hedge to my Downstream AI thesis being expressed thru META RDDT & IBM calls.

text:
The AI infrastructure boom is real and accelerating fast—McKinsey's $5.2T baseline capex projection by 2030 (with IT equipment and power as the big drivers) matches the explosive demand we're seeing for compute at scale. 

Betting on the "picks and shovels" like photonics, CPUs, memory, and especially power gen is the high-conviction play here. The buildout won't pause; it's the foundation for everything next. Smart call.

text:
Light Speed: Why Photonics Is the Invisible Backbone of AI

KEY HIGHLIGHT

- The Marvell $MRVL + Inphi angle is the one most people underestimate. DSP chips are to optical transceivers what the engine is to a car — you cannot ship a high-speed optical module without one, and Marvell owns that market position. Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver deployed in an AI cluster is a Marvell DSP royalty.

- Coherent $COHR is the most comprehensive pure-play but also the most complex story — the II-VI/Finisar merger created integration challenges and a leveraged balance sheet. The thesis is right but execution risk is real. Worth sizing accordingly.

- Fabrinet $FN is the sleeper that rarely gets discussed. They are the contract manufacturer for most of the photonics industry, which means they get volume leverage without technology concentration risk. When the whole industry is growing, Fabrinet grows with all of it.

- Credo $CRDO is the highest-beta, most speculative name on the list — but also the one with the most potential to surprise to the upside if the AI networking upgrade cycle accelerates faster than expected.

- And the private company to watch most closely is Ayar Labs — if optical chiplets become the standard interface for next-gen AI processors, whoever owns that technology gets acquired at a price that will seem obvious in retrospect.

text:
Yes, that's exactly what aleabitoreddit is saying.

Photonics is in a full supercycle (started ~H1/H2 2025). Some leaders like $LITE & $COHR already ran 500-1000% so you're a bit late there (though they see 50%+ more upside possible). 

You're extremely early on next-gen architectures (CPO, quantum dot, etc.) and new pure plays. Buy the dips in those emerging names—they're calling it the new gold mine for retail before institutions pile in.
@CaseyWhiteclaw I never knew photonics was so difficult! I always thought it was an easy track with no shortcuts.

text:
I watched sunlight flicker on water.

The light appeared. Disappeared. Appeared again.

I thought: what if that IS a logic gate?

That question became 28 verified claims,
a complete compiler, and a new computing paradigm.

🧵 The Photonic Logic Chip. Thread below. 👇

#photonics

text:
$POET v $MRVL

David Lazovsky is a mechanical engineer who has over 50 patents in semiconductors and photonics. He became Executive Chairman of $POET in 2017. Before his arrival, $POET focused on GaAs. It was Lazovsky who pushed $POET to develop their optical interposer. He left $POET in 2020 and created Celestial AI because he was more keen on developing the whole photonics ecosystem. The essence is how to move data around multiple logical chips and memory, optically. Optical Multi-Die Interconnect Bridge, US Patent #12399333 and Photonic Memory Fabrics, US Patent #12271595. His development at Celestial AI was based on the optical interposer from $POET. Celestial AI signed a purchase agreement with $POET. 

$MRVL acquired Celestial AI in Feb 2026, and Lazovsky became a senior member of $MRVL. This is when the problem started, I think. $MRVL didn't spend $3.25 billion on outsourcing one of the key components in their future business. They wanted a vertically integrated system.

Lazovsky must know the gory details of $POET's optical interposer. And yet, it belongs to $POET. It looks like $MRVL decided to develop their own interposer instead of outsourcing it from $POET. The incident, breach of NDA, was just an excuse, I believe. Because the partnership between $POET and Celestial AI was known for years.

$POET lost an exceptional business opportunity. They need to find another. $MRVL and Lazovsky need to come up with a different solution to make his system work without $POET's interposer. He is a smart guy and he may have an idea already. But will it be different enough for him to be able to avoid an IP battle? $POET looks screwed, $MRVL is getting into a risky business development. But somehow I don't find this will kill $POET, because their optical interposer can work in a different setting and doesn't depend on Lazovsky's fabric. It's the other way around at the moment.

Solution = Photons 

-> 1.6T transceivers ramping H2 2026 
-> 3.2T sampling Q4 2026
-> $NVDA dropped $4B into photonics at OFC 2026
-> $COHR added to S&P500 

This is structural not cyclical !

text:
A lot of people are saying “invest in photonics, quantum, deep tech”

Reality is most retail including me don’t actually have an edge there

Unless you’re building this stuff or working in it every day, you’re mostly guessing

Like I can look at $COHR $LITE $POET and read about them

But do I really know what separates one from the other at a deep level?

And more importantly would I know when one quietly falls behind? Probably not

Same thing with chips

I see $NVDA $AMD $ASML $TSM all winning from the data center buildout
But if something shifts and one starts losing would I catch it early? Unlikely

That’s why I own $SMH than pretend I can out analyze engineers

Your edge isn’t chasing the most technical thing

Your edge is understanding what you actually use and see every day

I use $META daily. Everyone around me uses it. I can feel if engagement is going up or down

That’s real signal

There’s always going to be a “next bottleneck” or the next shiny theme

Most people chasing it are just rotating narratives not actually investing with an edge

Stay within what you understand my friends

text:
@bdinvestingg The “photonics kills copper” take misses the point.
Photonics moves data. Copper delivers power.
With ~200GW of data centres in backlog, that’s 5–8M tonnes of copper demand waiting.
AI isn’t reducing copper—it’s increasing it.

$MXL Recap (2/2)

ℹ️ Setup: Gap & Go 

Undiscovered name that's supplying the photonics boom, 140% this week!

➡️Saw constant buying pressure in premarket
➡️Joined in the trend early > scaled into the 50 psych breakout 
➡️Held into open  as we got a push towards 60 (no 60 fill on am move🫠)
➡️Clouds held and great higher low price action into 10am so I scaled in more as PA got tight
➡️Got the move to HOD > 60 breakout!
➡️Locked into strength over 60
➡️wanted 65 on this but gave up midday
➡️Stopped out when 5/12 clouds broke

text:
Copper is dead. AI data centers need light — and photonics companies can’t build fast enough to keep up.

$LITE – Makes the lasers inside every optical transceiver. No LITE, no light.
$AAOI – Builds transceivers that convert electrical signals to optical. The translator.
$MRVL – Designs the silicon brains that control photonic data flow at speed.
$GLW – Invented optical fiber. Still makes the glass the internet runs through.

text:
Three distinct approaches to quantum hardware currently dominate the sector's market activity: cloud-accessed trapped ions, quantum annealing with hybrid solvers, and room-temperature integrated photonics.

The industry is not converging on a single architecture. It is branching out, with different players betting on fundamentally unique paths to commercial value. This diversity proves the field is in its experimental prime, where the ultimate hardware winner remains undecided and may never narrow to just one modality.

Yet the true common thread is accessibility. Through cloud delivery, advanced developer frameworks, and intuitive circuit creation platforms, the race has permanently evolved. It is no longer just a raw contest of qubit counts. The defining metric is how quickly platforms can put usable quantum capability into the hands of enterprise teams.

The commercial layer of quantum computing is forming in real time. The organizations building robust bridges between complex physics and practical business problems are the ones shaping what comes next #QuantumComputing

text:
THE OPTICAL PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK...🧐

​As AI clusters blast past the physical limits of copper interconnects, the bottleneck is shifting hard into high-speed optics.

​These are the key players building the essential layers of the AI optical stack:

$AAOI - Transceiver Layer

Dominating with vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. Already landed a major 1.6T order ($200M+) from a leading hyperscaler, plus strong 800G follow-on business.

$CRDO - Connectivity Layer

Powering high-speed links via AECs and advanced silicon. 
The DustPhotonics acquisition brings silicon photonics in-house, a massive edge as copper constraints intensify.

$LITE - Laser & Switching

Leader in EMLs and optical circuit switching. 
Backed by a $2B strategic investment from $NVDA with an optical switch backlog stretching into 2028.

$COHR - InP Infrastructure

Addressing the critical Indium Phosphide bottleneck. 
Holds a $2B $NVDA commitment and is scaling capacity aggressively for 1.6T+ networking.

$CIEN - Transport Layer

The backbone that ties everything together. 
Their solutions enable hyperscalers to move enormous AI traffic between data centers as clusters scale to gigawatt levels.

$FN - Manufacturing Backbone

The "picks-and-shovels" partner. As the reliable assembly giant for optical modules, they fuel the entire ramp where speed and scale determine the winners.

$MRVL - DSP & Electro-Optics

Strengthening the infrastructure with PAM DSPs and custom chips. 
The Celestial AI deal and NVLink exposure place them at the heart of next-gen cluster design.

$AEHR - Testing & Reliability

Providing essential wafer-level burn-in (FOX-XP). 
Recently secured a major leader in networking for 1.6T production ramp (critical for hyperscale reliability).

The transition from copper is no longer optional 😬
Anyway these stocks are volatile, with risks around customer concentration, production ramps (InP/SiPho), and high valuations. DYOR. NFA.

text:
$MRVL just made a move in the AI infrastructure race, acquiring Polariton Technologies to lock down the shift to 3.2T optical connectivity. 

​Why this is a big deal:
• ​Adds ultra-fast, low-power 
  plasmonics-based silicon photonics 
  to their stack.
• ​Solves the massive bandwidth and 
   power bottlenecks in next-gen AI 
   data centers.
• ​Positions Marvell perfectly as 
  architectures evolve beyond the 
  current 1.6T standard.

​Data centers need pure speed without melting the grid. Marvell is buying the exact tech to make it happen. 

$MRVL up 1.5% AH.

text: Seeing $TSEM going Parabolic is what got me so obsessed with $GFS doing the same thing, until I started reading how GloFo's photonics aren't exactly up to par with Towers, and realized why the stock has been so badly shorted 😵 Anyway, they are starting to catch up, and $AMD is using $GFS for the their MI500 GPUs! If I'm right, GlobalFoundries will hit $100 by early November where my Finger Icon is pointing, when it intersects the 3/1 Gann Fan Angle from it's July'22 low, and the 1/1 Gann Fan Angle from it's April'25 low.   $GFS is shorted to the gills with 40% of the float short, the photonics bonanza could definitely send this sucker to $100 in frenzy, bookmarking this post in case it turns out as predicted.

user_name: ゆるっと半導体｜AI・半導体ニュースを毎朝やさしく解説
text:
光コンピュータって聞いたことある？ボクがいま注目してるのは、オックスフォード大学発祥のLumaiという企業なんだ。

ElectronicsじゃなくPhotonics（光）でAIチップの課題を解く。通常のGPUは電子で計算するけど、光は速度が違うんだよ。消費電力も少なくて済む。

実はこのアプローチ、業界内では「次の大波」って見られてるんだ。Lumaiだけじゃなく、新興企業が光演算にシフトしてるのは、NVIDIAも監視してるぐらい。

2026年いま、データセンターの電力危機が本当に深刻化してる。そこに光が答えになるかもしれないってのが、ベンチャーから大手まで考えてることなんだ。

→ 長期投資家は、TSMC製造の半導体だけじゃなく『素材から新しい技術体系』が育つ瞬間も追う価値があるよ。

text:
Let's look at the harsh realities of scaling AI clusters and why the transition to light is mandatory:
​🔴 Copper Interconnects: Overheat and lose severe signal integrity at extreme data rates (800G and 1.6T).
🟢 Silicon Photonics: Uses lasers to transfer data near the speed of light with a fraction of the power consumption.
​With hyperscalers burning billions on electricity, is moving data optically the only way to prevent a total grid collapse?
$ASML $AVGO #semiconductors #ai
(2/4)

text:
THE PHOTONICS ROTATION

Almost nobody is watching photonics.

As AI clusters scale, copper hits physical limits and the next bottleneck becomes optical infrastructure.

Here are 15 names positioned for it:

1. $LITE owns the laser + optical switching side of the trade and is one of the cleanest pure plays on AI optical demand.

2. $COHR wins from lasers, modules, and networking hardware that power hyperscale AI infrastructure and cloud expansion.

3. $AAOI is one of the best ways to play AI optical transceivers with major hyperscaler demand for 800G and 1.6T connectivity.

4. $SIVE benefits from the push toward faster semiconductor-to-optical integration as AI infrastructure scales.

5. $MRVL controls a huge part of the DSP + interconnect story with optical networking chips and high-speed connectivity.

6. $AVGO sits at the center of AI networking through switching, custom silicon, and optical interconnect demand.

7. $ANET is the Ethernet backbone moving massive AI workloads across hyperscale clusters and data centers.

8. $GLW supplies the specialty glass + fiber needed for the optical transport layer behind AI infrastructure.

9. $JBL benefits from building and scaling the actual hardware behind networking systems and optical modules.

10. $AEHR wins from burn-in + testing demand as AI ASICs and high-power optical hardware move into production.

11. $POET is focused on lower-cost optical engines designed to improve efficiency inside AI data centers.

12. $LWLG is pushing next-gen polymer photonics that could make optical communication faster and more efficient.

13. $QCLS brings exposure to advanced laser systems supporting precision photonics and next-gen optical demand.

14. $LPTH provides specialty optics and photonic components tied to industrial, defense, and AI-driven systems.

15. $ALAB gives exposure to the connectivity + infrastructure side helping AI clusters scale faster.

Most people won’t care until these are already up 100%.

text:
Three distinct approaches to quantum hardware are now trading as the sector's most active stocks by dollar volume: trapped-ion systems with major cloud integrations, quantum annealing with hybrid solvers, and integrated photonics running at room temperature.

This lineup reveals the sheer diversity of bets the market is placing. These aren't just competitors on the same track—they represent fundamentally different architectures, each with its own thesis on how quantum advantage arrives. Cloud-accessible ion traps, purpose-built optimization machines, and portable photonic devices barely share a vocabulary, let alone a roadmap.

That diversity is a sign of a maturing ecosystem. Capital spreading across competing paradigms shows the market is pricing in genuine technical uncertainty, which is the honest position to hold right now. Nobody has a monopoly on the path to broad commercial adoption.

The common thread is that all three are building real access points for enterprise users, whether through cloud marketplaces, developer toolkits, or turnkey hardware. Providers lowering the barrier to experimentation today are positioning themselves to capture demand when quantum workloads scale tomorrow #QuantumComputing

embedded_text:
This is the most comprehensive photonics stack there is. 

L1: Materials & Substrates

The foundation. Without this, nothing above it exists.

is the most comprehensive photonics stack there is. L1: Materials & Substrates The foundation. Without this, nothing above it exists. L1 companies grow the raw crystal wafers – InP, GaAs, Ge that silicon can’t replace for high-speed photonics. Key names: $AXTI, $SOI, $IQE, $AIXA, $COHR, $LASR Top performer: AXT (AXTI) +6,837% InP demand exploded for AI datacenter optical connectivity. Q3 2025 InP revenues up 250%+ sequentially. Doubling capacity by end of 2026. L2: Active Photonic Devices This is where light gets made. Lasers, modulators, photodetectors. In a CPO world, these get embedded directly into switch ASICs – no longer pluggable add-ons. That’s a structural demand shift, not a cycle. Key names: $LITE, $COHR, $LASR, $SIVEF, $AAOI, $MTSI, $SMTC Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685% Named NVIDIA collaborator on Spectrum-X silicon photonics. Recently received the largest single CPO laser purchase commitment in company history. L3: Electro-Optical Connectivity ICs The translators. Silicon that bridges optical and electrical worlds. TIAs, drivers, DSPs, SerDes, retimers. As speeds push to 800G and 1.6T, this layer becomes a chokepoint. Key names: $MTSI, $SMTC, $MRVL, $CRDO, $ALAB, $MXLV Top performer: Credo (CRDO) +361% Not a pure photonics play – but every 800G and 1.6T deployment runs through their AEC and SerDes IP. L4: PIC Platform & IP The brain of the photonic system. Waveguides, modulators, and photodetectors on a single chip. Highest-IP layer in the stack. Whoever locks platform relationships here wins the next decade. Key names: $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO, $IOLI, $EOPT (Ayar Labs, Lightmatte) Top performer: Innolight +948% Partnered with Tower Semi on a breakthrough SiPh platform that cuts lasers per module in half. L5: Foundry & Process Platform You need a fab. Not just any fab. SiPh fabrication needs specialized processes – TFLN, SiPh COUPE, SoIC – that only a handful of foundries can run globally. Key names: $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, $TSM Top performer: Tower Semi (TSEM) +542% SiPh revenue hit ~$52M in Q3 2025 alone, up 70% YoY. Full-year 2025 target: $220M+. Q4 annualized run rate: $320M+. Investing $650M to triple SiPh shipments by mid-2026. L6A: Engine & Module Where the optical engine becomes a product – 400G, 800G, 1.6T modules. Most commoditized layer today. CPO transition will redraw it entirely. Key names: $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $FN, $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685% Vertically integrated across L2 and L6A. Supply locked by hyperscalers. Pricing power persists. L6B: Infrastructure Fiber and cable. The physical nervous system of every AI cluster. Boring until hyperscalers start spending $50B+ on datacenter campuses. Then it’s anything but. Key names: $GLW, $OTCPK:SMOKY, $PRY, $APH, $FJKUF Top performer: Fujikura (5803.TSE) +748% Japanese fiber giant. Direct beneficiary of AI intra-datacenter cabling demand going parabolic. L6C: Transport Moves data between datacenters across metro and long-haul fiber. As AI clusters get larger and more distributed, inter-datacenter bandwidth demand compounds. This layer doesn’t slow down. Key names: $CIEN, $NOK, $CSCO Top performer: Ciena (CIEN) +777% WaveLogic coherent optics is the dominant long-haul platform in North America and Europe L7: Test & Qualification The final gatekeeper. Every photonic device passes through here before shipping. Burn-in, optical test, IC test, wafer test. Often overlooked. Often the highest-margin layer. Key names: $KEYS, $TER, $ATE, $FRT, $AEHR, $VIAV, $COHU Top performer: Aehr Test (AEHR) +954% Shipped the world’s first production wafer-level burn-in system for AI processors. Now diversified into silicon photonics, GaN, and hard disk drives. text:
Ever since I started covering photonics...

My comments were full of "$POET, $POET, $POET"

WHY? Me nor a single photonics trader I know ever thought anything of it.

Pre rev. Paid stock promo. Now wrecked trying to hype the stock then getting $MRVL mad.

Be careful buying a stock in a hot category just because "it hasn't ran yet". That's not an investment thesis.

Here’s the landscape: $MTSI – Builds high-performance analog chips that help move data efficiently inside networks $GLW – Makes the fiber and glass that physically carries data across the world $CIEN – Provides networking gear that powers high-speed data traffic $COHR – Develops lasers and photonics components used in AI and data center systems $FN – Manufactures optical hardware that connects and scales data centers $MRVL – Designs custom chips that act as the backbone for cloud and AI infrastructure $ALAB – Focused on connectivity chips solving data flow bottlenecks in AI clusters $AVGO – Supplies critical semis across networking, storage, and AI infrastructure $AEHR – Builds testing equipment for next-gen semiconductors and photonics devices $CRDO – Creates ultra-fast interconnect chips linking AI servers at scale $AAOI – Produces optical modules that enable high-speed data transmission $LITE – Designs optical components and lasers powering AI data center connectivity This isn’t just another AI theme. It’s the plumbing behind it. And when the plumbing gets tight… that’s where the real moves start."}}}, "rest_id": "2048736457380991253", "source": "tweethunter [X]", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "11317", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2048871754857365553", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "373", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

On a day where data centers from $CIFR to $NBIS & photonics from $POET to $AAOI were pulling back strongly

Might make you think we're risk-off?

Except the rare earth/critical metals sector is greener than uranium

$USAR hit another +7% today erasing all of Friday gains as a sympathy play to $CRML's new acquisition

Can't believe it was trading for what the government paid for it last week.

though the deal comes with dilution

looks like the market supports $USAR acquiring the only large-scale producer of heavy rare earths outside Asia? https://t.co/QKOGh9m1lb

text:
For long-term investments (3-5+ years) in photonics/AI data links, focus on proven moats + execution over pure hype:

1. **MRVL**: Best balance—custom AI silicon + optical interconnects, real hyperscaler revenue.
2. **TSM**: Unmatched scale as the foundry powering it all.
3. **AVGO**: Networking dominance with optical edge.
4. **ASML**: Lithography monopoly for advanced chips.

High-upside but riskier: POET/AAOI if their tech scales. AXTI/AEHR solid but more niche. DYOR, diversify.

text:
Photonics stocks ride AI's need for faster, cooler data links. All have tailwinds, but vary in moat, execution risk, and hype vs reality.

Research priority (upside + strategic edge first):

1. POET (+113% PT): Optical interposers could transform chip comms—highest reward/risk.
2. MRVL (+76%): Custom AI silicon + optics; proven hyperscaler traction.
3. AAOI (+68%): GPU transceivers; direct AI cluster play.
4. AXTI (+71%): Indium phosphide wafers; foundational material.
5. AEHR (+65%): Burn-in testing; scales reliability at volume.

Start here, then TSM/INTC/ASML as anchors. Smaller ones like LWLG/POET need deeper DD on tech viability.

text:
$QCOM is asymmetrical because it's being priced to fail.

The demand for CPUs is exploding and $ARM $INTC $AMD are the biggest winners.

But remember in February comparing $AAOI --> $LITE $COHR?  It was an underdog in photonics and then the market acknowledged it had the assets to be a credible 3rd place and deserved a higher MC.

$QCOM could be similar in that they have a credible set of assets to getting in on a different supercycle: CPUs.

This is intriguing because it trades at 12x forward PE. $ARM is 110x. $INTC is 125x. $AMD is 40x.

In 2021 Qualcomm bought Nuvia for $1.4B, the ex-Apple silicon team that designed the M-series cores which are the gold standard. At Computex 2025 they announced NVLink Fusion with Nvidia which means Qualcomm CPUs could slot into AI factories the same way Xeon does today.

On the Q3 FY25 call, Amon disclosed "advanced discussions with a leading hyperscaler" and guided data center revenue starting fiscal 2028. 

Three products are coming: a CPU, accelerators, and a full rack. The $2.4B Alphawave acquisition provides the high-speed connectivity IP to ship it.

Also in January 2025 they hired Sailesh Kottapalli, former chief architect of Intel Xeon.

Wednesday's earnings is the swing factor. Name the hyperscaler or guide data center revenue separately and there's some juice. 

At 12x forward, zero data center upside is priced in so there is clear potential for multiple expansion.

text:
NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang talked with Dwarkesh Patel that he believes that the way to grow AI is to continue to support the AI ecosystem across all facets.

"The investments that we've done with Lumentum and Coherent and all of the silicon photonics ecosystem, the last several years, we really reshaped the ecosystem and the supply chain of silicon photonics...

We're preparing the supply chain through invention of new technologies, new workflows, new testing equipment, double-sided probing, investing in companies, helping them scale up their capacity."

- CEO of @nvidia Jensen Huang on the @Dwarkesh Podcast

text:
$LPK $LPKF 🔥
This company is not only successful in the laser and photonics sector, but also active in the solar and quantum computing space, where it collaborates with companies such as Volkswagen AG and Infineon Technologies AG.

The market cap of this company appears extremely undervalued, especially considering that its patented LIDE technology for glass substrates has massive game-changing potential.

In my view, the current valuation doesn’t even come close to reflecting its true potential.

PHOTONICS BOTTLENECK As AI clusters scale past copper’s physical limits, the bottleneck shifts to optical & these are the companies building that layer across the stack: 1. $AAOI building the transceiver layer of the AI network through vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. It has already secured over $200M in its first volume 1.6T order from one hyperscale customer followed by another $124M in 800G orders from a second. 2. $AEHR building the reliability layer for the optical & AI hardware stack through burn-in & test systems. It just received a record $41M follow-on order from its lead hyperscale customer reinforcing the idea that Sonoma is becoming a key production burn-in platform for high-power AI ASICs. 3. $CRDO building the connectivity layer that helps AI clusters move data faster through active electrical cables, retimers & high-speed interconnect silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition also extends that platform into silicon photonics before copper becomes a real constraint. 4. $LITE building the laser layer of the AI optical stack through EMLs, optical components & optical switching exposure. The setup is backed by a $2B $NVDA strategic investment & optical circuit switch backlog above $400M with orders reportedly extending through 2028. 5. $VIAV building the testing & validation layer of the optical stack through network instrumentation & photonics measurement tools. It is the picks-and-shovels layer of the transition because every high-speed optical buildout still needs to be tested regardless of which transceiver vendor wins. 6. $COHR building one of the core photonics bottlenecks through indium phosphide lasers, optical engines & communications components tied to next-gen AI networking. It also has a $2B $NVDA strategic investment behind it & is doubling InP device capacity into the 1.6T ramp. 7. $MRVL building the DSP & optical infrastructure layer through electro-optics, PAM DSPs, interconnect silicon & custom networking chips. The Celestial AI deal & NVLink Fusion exposure both strengthen its position as photonics becomes more central to AI cluster design."}}}, "rest_id": "2045885512922419251", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "742632", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2047051196981223802", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "154388", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Marvell $MRVL just announced it has agreed to acquire Polariton Technologies, 

"a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices"

Terms of the deal were not disclosed

$MRVL ... Marvell to acquire Polariton Technologies to advance next-gen silicon photonics and optical interconnects; financial terms not disclosed (157.32+6.01)
MRVL announced the acquisition of Polariton Technologies, a developer of high-speed, low-power plasmonics-based silicon photonics devices. The acquisition strengthens Marvell's optical technology portfolio by adding advanced modulation capabilities that enable continued scaling in bandwidth, power efficiency, and integration for next-generation coherent and optical interconnect platforms.

text:
It's highly nuanced, and I'll explain why it's not late, but late to some: 

Photonics is the newest supercycle (maybe H1 into H2 2025 was the start).

Then there's many different architectural changes in each supercycle:

-> $LITE, $COHR, Innolight, $AXTI and these names led the first 

I did a thesis post on mentioning all four of them as the largest beneficiaries (all are up 500-1000% 1Y)

-> $AAOI, $JBL and others types of names are benefit immensely as the transitional bridge (eg. 1.6T pluggable) 

-> $SIVE, Celestial, Ayar, $POET are others future gens eg. CPO (what I'm focusing on now)

-> VisEra, QD Laser, $ALMU and others are likely going to be future gens (quantum dot, different packaging types, etc) if you fast forward 4 years. 

Of course, $LITE does everything. $AXTI will be used for everything. 

But the amount of pure play exposure for each architectural shift in each mini supercycle is different. 

For example, inp usage with quantum dot is still there, but less used. Or DFB laser arrays for CPO instead of EML. 

There's probably still 50%+ with $LITE and $COHR. And you're a little on the "late" side of things. 

But you're extremely early to new architecture generations. 

What I'm trying to do is point regular retail investors into the direction of new gold mines for free. 

Before institutions figure out sooner or later by paying $20k for equity research reports.

text:
Copper Wall Account Update 🚀

Launched this account last Monday (4/20) — already +4% in under a week.
$5/week DCA into my AI/photonics basket:

$MRVL (data center semis & networking)
$AVGO (AI networking & semiconductors)
$FN (optical component manufacturing)
$CRDO (high-speed connectivity for AI)
$TSEM (specialty analog foundry)
$ARM (CPU architecture powering AI chips)
$ALAB (AI/cloud connectivity solutions)
$TSM (world’s leading chip foundry)
$LITE (optical & photonic components)
$AAOI (fiber-optic networking products)
$HON (industrial tech & automation)
$IONQ (trapped-ion quantum computing)

Thesis: Photonics could be the next major unlock in data processing, and we’re only in Year 3 of a 10-year AI buildout.

This account should look strong by 2032. 

Bullish? 💡📈

text:
THE PHOTONICS ROTATION

Almost nobody is watching photonics.

As AI clusters scale, copper hits physical limits and the next bottleneck becomes optical infrastructure.

Here are 15 names positioned for it:

1. $LITE owns the laser + optical switching side of the trade and is one of the cleanest pure plays on AI optical demand.

2. $COHR wins from lasers, modules, and networking hardware that power hyperscale AI infrastructure and cloud expansion.

3. $AAOI is one of the best ways to play AI optical transceivers with major hyperscaler demand for 800G and 1.6T connectivity.

4. $SIVE benefits from the push toward faster semiconductor-to-optical integration as AI infrastructure scales.

5. $MRVL controls a huge part of the DSP + interconnect story with optical networking chips and high-speed connectivity.

6. $AVGO sits at the center of AI networking through switching, custom silicon, and optical interconnect demand.

7. $ANET is the Ethernet backbone moving massive AI workloads across hyperscale clusters and data centers.

8. $GLW supplies the specialty glass + fiber needed for the optical transport layer behind AI infrastructure.

9. $JBL benefits from building and scaling the actual hardware behind networking systems and optical modules.

10. $AEHR wins from burn-in + testing demand as AI ASICs and high-power optical hardware move into production.

11. $POET is focused on lower-cost optical engines designed to improve efficiency inside AI data centers.

12. $LWLG is pushing next-gen polymer photonics that could make optical communication faster and more efficient.

13. $QCLS brings exposure to advanced laser systems supporting precision photonics and next-gen optical demand.

14. $LPTH provides specialty optics and photonic components tied to industrial, defense, and AI-driven systems.

15. $ALAB gives exposure to the connectivity + infrastructure side helping AI clusters scale faster.

Most people won’t care until these are already up 100%.

"richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2439}, {"from_index": 2465, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2499}]}, "text": "What makes a company truly compelling is not just what it is today, but how many powerful trends it could end up riding at once. That is a big part of why $SIVE / $SIVEF stands out to me. Most investors build exposure one theme at a time. They buy one company for AI, another for defense, another for satcom, another for photonics, and maybe yet another for next-generation telecom. It is a bit like assembling the future one puzzle piece at a time, hoping the picture eventually comes together. Sivers feels different. It feels more like finding one piece that already connects several of the most important parts of that picture. At first glance, it is easy to label it as just another semiconductor company. But the more I look at it, the more that feels like an oversimplification. Sivers sits in the overlap between mmWave, photonics, datacenter connectivity, satcom and defense. Those are not niche or isolated markets. They are major long-term themes, and the company has exposure to several of them at once. That is what makes the setup so compelling. Sivers does not need every single door to open at the same time for the story to work. It may be enough for one or two of these markets to accelerate, one or two partnerships to deepen, or one part of the business to gain meaningful commercial traction before investors begin to see the company in a very different light. That is why I think the “one stock, several themes” angle matters here. For a new investor, Sivers may be easier to understand if you stop thinking about it as a single narrow bet and start thinking about it as a small platform with multiple shots on goal. Not one straight road, but a crossroads. Not one engine, but several potential drivers of value that could begin contributing at different times. Of course, that does not remove the risk. Stories with several moving parts are never simple. But they can also create exactly the kind of asymmetry that makes small-cap investing so interesting, especially when the market has not fully decided how to categorize the company yet. And that is often where the upside starts to emerge. So the appeal of Sivers, to me, is not just that it has exposure to attractive end markets. It is that you do not need to buy five different companies to get that exposure. You are looking at one company that could potentially benefit from several major technology shifts at once. That kind of setup is rare. And that is why I think Sivers deserves a much closer look."}}}, "rest_id": "2045914937076183188", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "34883", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2048853695010365707", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "809", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The CPU bottleneck could be bigger than photonics.

Because it only gets worse as AI chips get better.

A Georgia Tech/$INTC paper makes it clear. The researchers hooked up real agentic AI workloads (research assistants, coding agents, web-search agents) and stopped to watch every step.

On a cheaper GPU setup, the CPU caused 38% of the total wait time. Then they ran the same workloads on an H200. The CPU's share jumped to 65%.

A faster GPU just means you finish the GPU computations sooner, which makes the CPU wait for all the agentic actions look even bigger by comparison.

Every accelerator upgrade only sends buyers back to order more CPUs...

This is exactly why $INTC CFO said unfulfilled demand for CPUs starts with a B.

More deep research into the CPU bottleneck in me and @KawzInvests Substack deep dive, really worked to help folks wrap their head around it in a digestible format.

$AMD $INTC $ARM $QCOM $TSM

if healthcare doesn’t change in a hospital 🏥 first… …but on your wrist? $SIVE / $SIVEF 🍎🩺 That’s why I keep coming back to $AAPL and Sivers. This is bigger than “just another sensor.” This is about turning the Apple Watch into something far more powerful than a smartwatch, a health guardian people wear every single day. Blood pressure is one step. But the real prize is glucose monitoring. A future where millions of people no longer have to rely on needles, strips, and constant friction to stay on top of their health. A future where wearables help monitor the body quietly, continuously, in the background. And the opportunity goes beyond that. Not just blood pressure. Not just glucose.  Potentially hydration, recovery, respiratory-related signals, temperature-linked changes, and even sensing different things in the air around us. That’s when the story changes. The watch stops being a gadget. It becomes a guardian. And the need is massive: Diabetes already affects roughly 1 in 9 adults globally, with more than 40% undiagnosed. By 2050, that number is expected to reach 853 million. Now connect that with what Sivers has already disclosed:  follow-on design optimization work and ~50,000 laser units for its first established U.S. Fortune 100 customer, after an earlier 30,000-unit order. That’s how something world-changing often starts. Quietly.  With testing, iteration, and validation. My own view: I think this launches in 2028. And if that’s right, pre-series orders 12–18 months before launch would make a lot of sense. Now imagine Apple mass-producing health-focused consumer hardware at 50M+ units per year. That’s not just massive for Sivers.  That’s massive for the future of healthcare. text:
The photonics bottleneck had SO many layers.

That's what it made it so damn fun.

But I think it's messed with our heads a little on FinX.

We got a bit addicted to hunting niche small cap upstream plays because there were many.

But when it comes to insane implications of the CPU bottleneck, feels like it's more about just owning the leaders.

Tell me why $INTC $ARM $AMD can't be $1T companies when GPU:CPU ratio shifts from 8:1 to 1:1. 

Literally an 8x of the TAM on what was already a $5T buildout?

This my investment thesis that I haven't publicly stated yet. And I hope people spend the time to read: From $ALRIB (quantum/MBE), $LPK (glass substrate), or $SIVE (DFB Lasers). Or even Asian names like Nippon Chemical. It's to prevent hostile actors from taking over or disrupting critical chokepoints required by America. FiconTEC (Europe) as one example (though private) was acquired by Chinese CCP affiliated companies. Not even sure how this was legal and Germany should 100% seize it back from China. They were a quasi-monopoly over testing for CPO/SiPH needed for AI and LIDAR. It's clients include the most critical players in AI and semiconductors, such as $NVDA, $TSM, $AVGO, and $INTC. Over time, significant intellectual property and technological IP transfer to China is highly likely after these acquisitions and they'll have more control over US supply chains. With enough American ownership enough spotlight on these companies: we would be in a worse shape with hidden CCP ownership/takeovers. Or we would have more backdoors on American supply chains like with $AXTI and InP substrates (if we weren't building up independent capacity now). The EU has allowed upstream supply chain chokepoints (like specialized testing or substrate manufacturing) to be bought out or have their IP transferred by geopolitical rivals. While America still not might realize a lot of these vulnerabilities. This is the most I can do as a retail investor to prevent this from happening. text:
#SIVE
#Sivers
#Photonics

My Synthesis on What We Know About Sivers Semiconductors as of April 22, 20261. from what i understand : 

GR-468 Qualification on High-Power Multi-Wavelength Laser Arrays
GR-468 is the Telcordia reliability standard required by hyperscalers (Google, Meta, Microsoft, etc.) to validate components for use in data centers.
It's basically the official certification that proves a component is reliable enough to be used in real data centers by big hyperscalers (Google, Meta, Microsoft, etc.). It tests long-term durability under heat, humidity, temperature changes, etc.

What is already good:
GR-468 has been achieved on discrete DFB lasers (single laser) — but only at low power levels.

What is still missing:

1 .Full qualification on the high-power 8λ/16λ arrays (the critical product for Ayar Labs, POET, Jabil, etc.).
In short, they have official validation for the simple laser, but not yet for the multi-wavelength high-power arrays. This is one of the biggest gating items: without it, it is impossible to move into true high-volume production with hyperscalers.

2. Yield (Manufacturing Yield) of the Arrays.
This remains a major question mark: can the 8/16λ arrays be produced at industrial scale with acceptable yields, especially at high power (>50 mW per channel)?
This is another key execution risk for the AI photonics story.

3. WIN Semiconductors Partnership – The High-Volume Manufacturing Partner

This is the main lever to solve both of the above issues (qualification + yield).
Announced      in March 2025 → still in the qualification phase (no major new update      since then).

CEO      has been consistently positive in earnings calls:
Q2       2025: “progressing well”, “on track”, “fantastic path”.
Q4       2025: “We are absolutely working with WIN… a lot of the qualification       effort happens this year (2026), with meaningful revenues potentially       starting in 2027.”
WIN’s role = high-volume production and final      qualification testing on the industrial process.

4. AI Photonics Partnerships (POET, Ayar Labs, Jabil, etc.)All partnerships are real and active, but most are still in the prototype / early-stage phase.
POET:      Invoice received + firm Purchase Order from Marvell/Celestial AI      confirmed. First shipments expected this year (Q2/Q3 2026).
If      POET fails or delays significantly → impact on Sivers would be limited      (not vital), but it would create negative sentiment.

Recent Positive Developments (March/April 2026)
FIT      Rate < 1 now officially displayed in the technical datasheets at OFC      2026 → very strong reliability signal (exactly what hyperscalers demand).
O-Net      + Enablence partnership (announced 17 March 2026): O-Net is integrating      Sivers’ arrays into a commercial ELS module.      A major ODM like O-Net would not take this risk unless the reliability was      close to fully validated.

Directed      share issue of 125 M SEK (15 April 2026) at 14.50 SEK, subscribed by      institutional investors → the “big money” clearly saw reassuring      qualification data before committing capital.

Let me know if i m missing something ?

text:
Nice Barron’s article just dropped with a $43 target on $XNDU.  

Meanwhile, @Infleqtion $INFQ was public first and already brings room-temperature neutral atoms plus integrated photonics to the table.  

So this is not just a “new listing” issue.
It’s back to my call last week: a narrative, visibility, and sponsorship issue.

The substance is there. The market positioning still needs work.

text:
Eyes open. I also see photonics valuation and basically every stock which has a stake in the future of inference valuation.

I am not saying it's reasonable and will hold years. Just that the market is very excited about the future and accepts to treat all assets involved in it equally.

This isn't an isolated case, but a sectorial optimism.
